      <title>Pelicans Hiring Jamahl Mosley As Next Head Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285691/Pelicans-Hiring-Jamahl-Mosley-As-Next-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>The New Orleans Pelicans are hiring Jamahl Mosley as their next head coach. New Orleans had delayed making a decision on a new coach until Mosley had parted ways with the Orlando Magic. It's been widely reported that Pelicans front office heads Joe Dumars and Troy Weaver are big fans of Mosley.</p>
<p>Mosley is reportedly getting a five-year contract to lead New Orleans.</p>
<p>In five seasons as the head coach of the Magic, Mosley went 189-221. Over the last three years, Mosley won 133 regular season games, while guiding Orlando to three consecutive playoff appearances.</p>
<p>The Pelicans believe their team is in a similar position to where the Magic were when Mosley took over. New Orleans is hoping that Mosley can spur a similar defensive improvement that Orlando had, as well as a similar climb up the standings.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 18 May 2026 11:47:41 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285691/Pelicans-Hiring-Jamahl-Mosley-As-Next-Head-Coach</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mosley_Jamahl_orl_231205.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joe Ingles Signs Two-Year Deal With Melbourne United</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285697/Joe-Ingles-Signs-Two-Year-Deal-With-Melbourne-United</link>
      <description><p>Joe Ingles has confirmed he will sign with Melbourne United on a two-year deal. Ingles began his career in 2006 with the South Dragons and won an NBL championship with the club.</p>
<p>After leaving Australia in 2009, Ingles spent three seasons playing in Spain for Granada and Barcelona before joining Maccabi Tel Aviv for the 2013-14 season. Ingles then left for the NBA ahead of the 2014-15 season when initially joining the Los Angeles Clippers before settling in with the Utah Jazz.</p>
<p>Ingles' tenure with the Jazz ended with a torn ACL in January 2022. Ingles went on to have stints with the Milwaukee Bucks and Orlando Magic before spending the past two seasons with the Minnesota Timberwolves. </p>
<p>"Coming home to Melbourne feels incredibly special," Ingles said.</p>
<p>"To return to where my professional career began almost 20 years ago is something I don't take for granted.</p>
<p>"So much has happened since then, but Melbourne has always felt like home.</p>
<p>"We're really excited to reconnect with the community here, and to be closer to family and friends again. After so many years overseas, that part means a lot to us."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 18 May 2026 21:58:59 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285697/Joe-Ingles-Signs-Two-Year-Deal-With-Melbourne-United</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Ingles_Joe_min_250630.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>De'Aaron Fox Out For Game 1 Of Western Conference Finals With Ankle Injury</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285695/DeAaron-Fox-Out-For-Game-1-Of-Western-Conference-Finals-With-Ankle-Injury</link>
      <description><p>De'Aaron Fox has been ruled out by the San Antonio Spurs for Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals due to a right ankle sprain. Fox has been dealing with the ankle injury since Game 4 of the second round when Ayo Dosunmu landed on his ankle.</p>
<p>Fox finished Game 4 and subsequently played in Games 5 and 6. Fox is averaging 18.2 points, 3.5 rebounds and 5.8 assist in 33.3 minutes over 11 playoff games this season.  </p>
<p>Fox started 72 games during the regular season and hasn't missed a game since March 25.</p>
<p>Rookie Dylan Harper figures to play a bigger role in the absence of Fox.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 18 May 2026 20:20:38 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285695/DeAaron-Fox-Out-For-Game-1-Of-Western-Conference-Finals-With-Ankle-Injury</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Fox_Dearon_sas_250206.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Darius Acuff Jr. Strongly Linked With Kings In Draft</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285696/Darius-Acuff-Jr-Strongly-Linked-With-Kings-In-Draft</link>
      <description><p>Darius Acuff Jr. has been strongly linked with the Sacramento Kings, who own the seventh overall pick. The Kings have a need at point guard and Acuff would give them a dynamic option at the position after trading away Tyrese Haliburton in 2022 and De'Aaron Fox in 2025.</p>
<p>Acuff's father played for Kings' general manager Scott Perry at Eastern Kentucky in the late 1990s. Acuff is represented by <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Justin-Haynes/206">Justin Haynes</a> and <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/info/agent_clients/Bill-Duffy/16">Bill Duffy</a>.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/player/Darius-Acuff-Jr/Summary/216187">Acuff averaged 23.5 points, 3.1 rebounds and 6.4 assists in 36 games in his freshman season at Arkansas</a>. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 18 May 2026 20:29:37 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285696/Darius-Acuff-Jr-Strongly-Linked-With-Kings-In-Draft</guid>

   <item>
      <title>Donovan Mitchell Says Cavs 'Can Breathe A Little Bit' Following Game 7 Win Over Pistons</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285686/Donovan-Mitchell-Says-Cavs-Can-Breathe-A-Little-Bit-Following-Game-7-Win-Over-Pistons</link>
      <description><p>The Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Detroit Pistons 125-94 in Game 7 to win the Eastern Conference semifinals on Sunday night.</p>
<p>Donovan Mitchell finished the game with 26 points, 8 assists, and 6 rebounds. Mitchell was responsible for 43 points on the night, with his teammates shooting 8-for-8 off his passes.</p>
<p>"Even last year, when we lost to Indiana, we had our goals set on getting to the Finals. We're just one step closer," Mitchell said. "But, yeah it's been almost a decade of running into the same issue. So for sure, I personally, and as a team, we can breathe a little bit."</p>
<p>Sam Merrill finished with 23 points off the bench, including five 3-pointers. Evan Mobley added 21 points and 10 rebounds, with Jarrett Allen getting 23 points and 7 rebounds.</p>
<p>"I've been saying all year we have a lot to prove," Cavs coach Kenny Atkinson said after the win. "We still have more to prove. That's the next part of it, but we proved something to ourselves, that we could take that another step."</p>
<p>The Cavs have lost in the second round of the playoffs the last two years and have not made a conference final since 2018.</p>
<p>Game 1 of the Eastern Conference Finals against the Knicks starts on Tuesday at Madison Square Garden. </p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 18 May 2026 01:55:34 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285686/Donovan-Mitchell-Says-Cavs-Can-Breathe-A-Little-Bit-Following-Game-7-Win-Over-Pistons</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mitchell_Donovan_cle_260501.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>NBA's Proposed Lottery Changes Could Increase First-Round Pick Value, Complicate Trade Market</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285679/NBAs-Proposed-Lottery-Changes-Could-Increase-First-Round-Pick-Value-Complicate-Trade-Market</link>
      <description><p>Beyond the debate over tanking, several NBA front office members are warning that the league's proposed 3-2-1 lottery system could produce unintended consequences that reshape how franchises build rosters. The concern centers on first-round pick values and an already difficult trade market.</p>
<p>Under the new format, flatter lottery odds would give virtually every first-round pick a meaningful chance at a high selection. A team picking 16th, which currently has no path to a top-three pick, would hold 2.7 percent odds at the No. 1 selection. That figure exceeds the 1.8 percent odds the Dallas Mavericks held last year when they won the right to select Cooper Flagg.</p>
<p>Executives predict that dynamic would make most first-round picks more valuable league-wide, creating friction in a transaction market already strained by first and second apron restrictions and a free agency landscape that has grown increasingly limited in recent years.</p>
<p>Teams may become reluctant to <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/draft/future_drafts/tradeable">trade future first-round picks</a> even when projecting strong records, knowing any pick carries legitimate lottery upside. That hesitancy could slow deal-making across the league.</p>
<p>"This system would take even more control out of a team's hands in how to rebuild," one assistant general manager told The Athletic.</p>
<p>A separate frustration stems from timing. Several executives expressed irritation that the proposed changes arrived without warning after their organizations traded away future first-round picks under the existing value system. Those picks were moved without any knowledge that the underlying lottery structure was about to shift fundamentally.</p>
<p>The NBA has framed the 3-2-1 proposal as a collaborative process, holding multiple meetings with general managers ahead of the May 28 Board of Governors vote. But for executives who built long-term plans around the current format, the abruptness of the potential shift carries real consequences.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 21:13:16 EDT</pubDate>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285678/Jalen-Williams-Available-For-Game-1-Of-Western-Conference-Finals</link>
      <description><p>The Oklahoma City Thunder have listed forward Jalen Williams as available to play in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals. The Thunder open the West Finals on Monday, when they host the San Antonio Spurs.</p>
<p>Williams has been out since April 22, when he suffered a strained hamstring during Game 2 of Oklahoma City's first-round series against the Phoenix Suns. Williams missed the remainder of that series and the second-round series against the Los Angeles Lakers. The Thunder swept both series to move to the Western Conference Finals.</p>
<p>This has been somewhat of a lost season for Williams to this point. He appeared in just 33 regular season games, and only two playoff games so far. The Thunder star forward started the year rehabbing from offseason wrist surgery. Then he suffered two hamstring strains which have sidelined him for a month-and-a-half and now nearly a month.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Nuggets Expected To Cut Costs This Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285677/Nuggets-Expected-To-Cut-Costs-This-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>The Denver Nuggets have no intention of blowing up their roster and starting over. However, the Nuggets are expected to trim some salary heading into next season.</p>
<p>Jonas Valanciunas isn't expected to be back in Denver next season. Valanciunas' $10 million contract is only guaranteed for $2 million prior to July 8. The Nuggets are expected to trade Valanciunas to a team that is willing to that $2 million guarantee, or Denver will waive Valanciunas and save $8 million before his deal becomes fully guaranteed.</p>
<p>The Nuggets may also explore trading Zeke Nnaji, who has a fully guaranteed $7.5 million deal. Denver could also look to trade Cameron Johnson in a salary-shedding deal. Johnson is heading into the final season of his contract at $23.1 million.</p>
<p>Denver will also face tough decisions with Peyton Watson and Spencer Jones, who are both restricted free agents this summer. Having their restricted rights gives the Nuggets a leg up on retaining either Watson or Jones. But the team's financial situation with the tax and aprons could force their hand, should either player receive a large offer sheet from another team.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 17:12:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285677/Nuggets-Expected-To-Cut-Costs-This-Offseason</guid>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Donovan Mitchell's Best Extension Option Is Waiting Until 2027, Complicating Cavs' Position</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285676/Donovan-Mitchells-Best-Extension-Option-Is-Waiting-Until-2027-Complicating-Cavs-Position</link>
      <description><p>Donovan Mitchell faces a significant contract decision this summer with the Cleveland Cavaliers, and the most advantageous one for him is complicated for the franchise. Mitchell is under no obligation to act before 2027, and that reality is a source of discomfort for the organization.</p>
<p>Mitchell's current deal pays him $50 million for 2026-27, with a player option worth $54 million for the following season. The Cavaliers would prefer to replace that option year with a new contract as early as July, when they could offer four years and up to $272 million.</p>
<p>But waiting serves Mitchell far better financially. With 10 years of NBA service time in 2027, he would be eligible to sign a five-year deal worth approximately $350 million, along with perks including a no-trade clause. That represents a substantial difference in total guaranteed money, particularly for a player who would be in his mid-30s by the time the additional season came into effect.</p>
<p>The tradeoff is that waiting would make Mitchell an unrestricted free agent, a cloud that would hang over the franchise throughout next season.</p>
<p>Mitchell has given Cleveland no reason for concern since arriving in 2022 via a trade with the Utah Jazz. He extended his contract in 2024 and has never publicly indicated any desire to leave. The Cavaliers understood this moment would arrive regardless of the team's performance when they agreed to that extension.</p>
<p>Since the Washington Wizards signed Bradley Beal to his five-year, $251 million contract with a no-trade clause, there has been doubt across the NBA that any players would receive a no-trade clause again on a contract of that length unless they were true perennial MVP candidates.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 13:30:43 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285676/Donovan-Mitchells-Best-Extension-Option-Is-Waiting-Until-2027-Complicating-Cavs-Position</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Mitchell_Donovan_cle_260501.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>James Harden, Cavaliers Expected To Sign Multi-Year Deal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285675/James-Harden-Cavaliers-Expected-To-Sign-Multi-Year-Deal</link>
      <description><p>James Harden is expected to return to the Cleveland Cavaliers on a multi-year contract this summer, sources told ESPN. Though a formal agreement cannot be completed until the offseason, an understanding is in place between both sides to work out a new deal.</p>
<p>Cleveland acquired Harden from the Los Angeles Clippers at the trade deadline, in part because the veteran guard sought contract certainty. The Clippers had not committed to his $42 million team option for next season, prompting his representatives to seek a team willing to make a longer commitment.</p>
<p>The Cavaliers were willing to make that commitment. Harden could have vetoed the trade, meaning both sides had established mutual interest before the deal was finalized.</p>
<p>A reduced annual salary in exchange for more guaranteed money over additional seasons could also help Cleveland manage its second-apron obligations. The Cavaliers carry the NBA's largest payroll at $226 million, exceeding $280 million including the luxury tax.</p>
<p>Kenny Atkinson has worked deliberately to build a strong relationship with Harden from their first interaction. When Cleveland posted Atkinson's victory speech following a Game 5 win in Detroit last week, Harden was seen addressing teammates from his locker.</p>
<p>Atkinson has drawn on the history of Harden's most productive seasons, which coincided with strong coaching relationships, most notably with Mike D'Antoni in Houston.</p>
<p>Harden has been inconsistent through the postseason but remains expected to return regardless of how Cleveland's second-round series against Detroit concludes.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 13:24:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285675/James-Harden-Cavaliers-Expected-To-Sign-Multi-Year-Deal</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Harden_James_cle_260420.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Wins 2nd Straight NBA Most Valuable Player Award</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285674/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Wins-2nd-Straight-NBA-Most-Valuable-Player-Award</link>
      <description><p>Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has won his second straight <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Most-Valuable-Player/1">NBA Most Valuable Player award</a>. Gilgeous-Alexander officially received the award on Sunday.</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander received 83 out of 100 first place votes, 13 second place votes, three third place votes and one fourth place vote. Nikola Jokic finished second with 10 first place votes, 48 second place votes, 37 third place votes, four fourth place votes and one fifth place vote. Victor Wembanyama finished third with five first place votes, 36 second place votes, 47 third place votes, 10 fourth place votes and two fifth place votes. Luka Doncic and Cade Cunningham rounded out the top-5. Cunningham received two first place votes. Jaylen Brown, Kawhi Leonard and Donovan Mitchell were the other tree players to receive votes.</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander becomes the 14th player in league history to win the award in consecutive seasons. Gilgeous-Alexander has finished in the top-5 in MVP voting in each of the past four seasons.</p>
<p>"It's special," Gilgeous-Alexander said of joining such exclusive company. "All those guys have shaped the game of basketball. All those guys have changed the game and how it's played and how it was approached before that. To be in just that circle, to be in that conversation, it's something that I don't take lightly. I'm super grateful for it."</p>
<p>The Oklahoma City Thunder led the NBA in wins and net rating for the second consecutive season.</p>
<p>Gilgeous-Alexander averaged 31.1 points, 4.3 rebounds and 6.6 assists with a PER of 30.5. Last season, Gilgeous-Alexander averaged 32.7 points, 4.3 rebounds and 6.6 assists while also posting a 30.5 PER.</p>
<p>Victor Wembanyama and Nikola Jokic were the other two finalists for the award.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 09:52:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285674/Shai-Gilgeous-Alexander-Wins-2nd-Straight-NBA-Most-Valuable-Player-Award</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Gilgeousalexander_Shai_okc_250602.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Wolves Pursuing Tim Connelly Contract Extension</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285673/Wolves-Pursuing-Tim-Connelly-Contract-Extension</link>
      <description><p>The Minnesota Timberwolves are moving to retain president of basketball operations Tim Connelly on a contract extension, team sources told The Athletic. Connelly enters the final year of his current deal following Minnesota's second-round exit against the San Antonio Spurs.</p>
<p>Despite being considered a candidate for the top basketball operations role in Dallas, there were no actual discussions between Connelly and the Mavericks before the franchise hired Masai Ujiri, team sources said.</p>
<p>General manager Matt Lloyd, who works directly under Connelly, came close to departing as well. Lloyd interviewed as a finalist for the Chicago Bulls' lead executive position but was passed over when the Bulls hired Bryson Graham from Atlanta.</p>
<p>Lloyd's decision to remain with Minnesota was welcomed throughout the organization. He is a well-regarded figure on both the basketball and business sides of the operation, and his continued presence has lifted the spirits of staff across departments.</p>
<p>Connelly and Lloyd are widely respected throughout the league. Their partnership has produced blockbuster trades for Rudy Gobert, Julius Randle, Donte DiVincenzo and Ayo Dosunmu, along with smaller but impactful acquisitions of Mike Conley and Nickeil Alexander-Walker.</p>
<p>Chris Finch has two years remaining on his contract and will return for a sixth full season. Minnesota's front office is expected to return intact, a meaningful source of organizational continuity as the Wolves face a challenging offseason with significant roster decisions ahead.</p>
<p>Connelly will enter extension talks with ownership firmly behind him despite three consecutive second-round exits and a widening gap in the Western Conference standings.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 09:33:31 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285673/Wolves-Pursuing-Tim-Connelly-Contract-Extension</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Connelly_Tim_den_220523.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Anthony Edwards Defers To Wolves' Management On Closing Gap With Spurs, Thunder</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285672/Anthony-Edwards-Defers-To-Wolves-Management-On-Closing-Gap-With-Spurs-Thunder</link>
      <description><p>Anthony Edwards had no clear answer Friday when asked how the Minnesota Timberwolves can close the distance between themselves and the Western Conference's two elite teams, the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs. The question, posed after a 139-109 Game 6 loss to San Antonio, landed without a clean response.</p>
<p>"I don't know man," said Edwards when asked what needs to happen for the Wolves to close the gap on the Spurs and Thunder. "I don't think that's a question for me."</p>
<p>Minnesota finished the season 13 wins behind second-seeded San Antonio and 15 behind defending champion Oklahoma City. Three of the Wolves' four losses to the Spurs came by margins of 38, 29 and 30 points.</p>
<p>Edwards played through injuries to both knees throughout the series, performing at roughly 70 percent of his normal athleticism by the team's own assessment. He scored 24 points in the finale despite the physical limitations.</p>
<p>Chris Finch was more direct in framing what the franchise must confront this offseason.</p>
<p>"You either gotta be a problem or have a solution," said Finch. "You have to be built in a way that troubles your opponent with something they don't have or have something to counter what they do have."</p>
<p>Edwards turns 25 in August and enters his prime. Minnesota's front office faces urgent decisions about how to upgrade the supporting cast around him before the window narrows further.</p>
<p>"I wasn't expecting to be going home this early," Edwards said, "so I haven't thought about what I'm going to add to my game."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 17 May 2026 09:30:24 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285672/Anthony-Edwards-Defers-To-Wolves-Management-On-Closing-Gap-With-Spurs-Thunder</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Edwards_Anthony_min_260420.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Naz Reid Says Wolves Need To Be 'Less Moody, More Selfless'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285671/Naz-Reid-Says-Wolves-Need-To-Be-Less-Moody-More-Selfless</link>
      <description><p>In the wake of the Minnesota Timberwolves loss to the San Antonio Spurs in the second round of the NBA Playoffs, Naz Reid was asked what his team needs to do to catch the Spurs and the Oklahoma City Thunder. Reid was very direct in his answer for how the Wolves run down the Western Conference finalists.</p>
<p>"Probably just the moodiness. You look at both of those teams and they're playing for one another, they're excited to be on the floor with one another, they're a team where they're selfless... I think we have more than enough talent... But just being less moody. I think that's just the name of the game for us, just being less moody and more selfless," Reed said.</p>
<p>Reid was one of several Timberwolves to directly address chemistry issues the team had this season. Minnesota star Anthony Edwards also said that his team didn't build championship habits throughout the regular season.</p></description>

      <title>Warriors Want Draymond Green To Return But Say Decision Is Up To Him</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285670/Warriors-Want-Draymond-Green-To-Return-But-Say-Decision-Is-Up-To-Him</link>
      <description><p>Veteran forward Draymond Green holds a player option for next season. That gives Green some flexibility with his future. The Golden State Warriors hope that future includes staying in the Bay Area.</p>
<p>"I think we have had discussions where we want him to finish his career (as) a Warrior," Golden State GM Mike Dunleavy said recently. "He kind of feels the same way. I would expect him to be back, but it's his call on that."</p>
<p>Newly-extended Warriors head coach Steve Kerr echoed Dunleavy's sentiments about Green.</p>
<p>"The number one thing is, it's up to him," Kerr said. "He has the player option, so he has to go through that with his agent, how that plays out. I am committed to coaching him, coaching Steph (Curry), obviously, for as long as they're here. I look at them as collaborators, and what we've built is pretty special."</p>
<p>Green is expected to opt out of his deal, but not necessarily to leave Golden State. Green is likely looking to add more years to his deal by re-signing with the Warriors, perhaps at lower figure than his $27.7 million player option. Green would make that up by adding more years with total guaranteed salary.</p></description>

      <description><p>Veteran point guard Mike Conley is shrugging off retirement talk for at least one more year. Conley said that his play in the playoffs proved to him that he can still play.  Now, Conley would like to play in a 20th NBA season next season.</p>
<p>Minnesota Timberwolves backcourt mate Bones Hyland said that he hopes to return to team next season. After bouncing around a bit, Hyland feels like he's found a home in Minnesota.</p>

      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285666/Jrue-Holiday-Hopes-To-Remain-With-Trail-Blazers</link>
      <description><p>Veteran guard Jrue Holiday has moved around a lot in recent years. In the last few years, Holiday has been traded from the Milwaukee Bucks to the Portland Trail Blazers to the Boston Celtics then back to the Trail Blazers. Now, Holiday hopes to stay in Portland long-term.</p>
<p>"I hope so. I don't like being traded and moved and stuff," Holiday said when asked if he hopes to stay with the Blazers. "I like being a part of something and building with the team and seeing the progress and going through the progress with them. So I hope so. I'd love to be here.</p>
<p>"Honestly, just kind of how we ended the season, fighting and clawing, knowing that we have a team that has proven ourselves to some extent to be a playoff team. We have a good mix of older gentlemen and then young guys that are still hungry. You hear them. They're still in the gym now and it's been two days (since) we lost in the playoffs. So I'm really excited for that."</p>
<p>Portland is entering the offseason with a logjam at the guard position. In addition to Holiday, the Trail Blazers have Scoot Henderson heading into the final season of his rookie scale contract and Shaedon Sharpe starting his four-year extension. The Blazers will also have Damian Lillard in the lineup, as he missed the entirety of last season while recovering from a torn Achilles' tendon.</p>
<p>That amount of talent in the backcourt, along with Holiday's significant salary, has led to speculation that Portland could look to move the veteran guard. If Holiday has his way, he'll stick around to help the Trail Blazers continue to climb up the Western Conference standings.</p></description>

<p>Minnesota could also see other movement in their backcourt. Mike Conley may retire after 19 NBA seasons. Bones Hyland is also a free agent and is coming off a bounce-back season that may have gotten his NBA career back on track.       <description><p>In the wake of falling the second round of the playoffs to the San Antonio Spurs, the Minnesota Timberwolves are expected to look at upgrading their roster. The Wolves are looking to put themselves on the level of the Spurs and the Oklahoma City Thunder as contenders in the Western Conference.</p>

Milwaukee is expected to more seriously entertain offers for the star forward this offseason.</p>
